titleStmt.title: Letter and Christmas card from Seán Mac Diarmada to Máirín Cregan, 23 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Seán Mac Diarmada

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Seán Mac Diarmada (1883-1916) was one of the key organisers of the Easter Rising. He signed the Proclamation of the Irish Republic and was executed in May 1916. The letter wishes Máirín a happy christmas season and apologises for being unable to meet her. Máirín is probably Mairín Cregan, a prolific Irish children's writer who married James Ryan in 1919: the letter survives in Ryan's personal papers. The letter is sent from the office of the newspaper 'Nationality', an organ of the Irish Republican Brotherhood. Mac Diarmada was the manager and registered owner of the newspaper.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
"Nationality" Ár dtír féin Offices :—
12 D'OLIER STREET,
Dublin, 23.12 1915 Dear Mairín, I'm enclosing a card to remind you of the
happy season and to say that I wish you all the
joys that life can give - the very best there is to be
found — let me say how sorry I am that I could not
have seen you on Monday evening. I then hoped to
see you on Monday night and explain but alas,
I was again disappointed. I console myself with
the firm belief that you understand I could not go. Life in Dublin, in that sense, is somewhat
miserable for the past few days. All the set are
gone. I have not seen any of them since Sunday
night. Oh yes I saw Miss Davin but at a
distance I was in a train & she was going
down Abbey St - not even within hailing distance. I hope you are having a very enjoyable
holiday Yours very sincerely Seán MacDiarmuida
NODLAIG AOIBHINN SHEUNMHAR SHUGHACH,
GO SEOLTAR CHUGHAT A CHARA CHAOMH,
FÉ BHEANNACHTGO RAIBH DO DHÚN,
FÉ MHEIDHIR DO BHUIDHEAN BHUIDH, 'S TÚ FÉIN.
Ó SEÁN MAC DIARMADA, Dublin Nodlaig, 1915
